world.title: South Africa Publishes Revised Rhino Conservation Management Plan
world.description: South Africa's Minister of Forestry, Fisheries and the Environment, Willie Aucamp, announced yesterday the publication of the revised Black and White Rhino Biodiversity Management Plan (BMP) in the Government Gazette.

world.title: New roads, artillery positions built in Belarus near Ukraine border, Zelensky says
world.description: "We believe that Russia will once again attempt to drag Belarus into its war," the Ukrainian president said on April 17.

world.title: Four arrested in latest ‘PowerOFF’ DDoS-for-hire takedown
world.description: More than 20 countries participated in a coordinated takedown of platforms selling cheap access to distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks.
world.source: The Record from Recorded Future News

world.title: How To Avoid Them
world.description: The new Entry and Exit System (EES) is causing long lines at airports in Europe, but a few practical tips can help you avoid the worst waits.

world.description: India's rail expertise can benefit South Africa: envoy

world.title: As Iran war fallout spreads, the focus shifts to Trump's mind - as it should
world.description: The world is stuck watching a performative series of announcements about meetings that could, may, will happen, involving a president not dealing with the reality that he is in a weak and confused bargaining position.

world.title: Trump Admits His Doordash Stunt Was ‘Tacky’
world.description: After accepting a McDonalds delivery from ‘DoorDash Grandma’ Sharon Simmons on Monday, Trump admitted the stunt was a bit ‘tacky’ and ‘embarrassing.’

world.title: UK and France to lead defensive mission in Strait of Hormuz
world.description: Sir Keir Starmer has said further details of the plan will come after a meeting in London next week.

business.title: U.S. Chamber Discusses How Medical Breakthroughs Are Extending Lives and Strengthening the Economy with YourUpdateTV
business.description: Nationwide media tour conducted by U.S. Chamber of Commerce, in conjunction with D S Simon Media, on topic of new analysis commissioned by the U.S. Chamber of Commerce revealing just how transformative American medical innovation has been for families, communities, and the economy.

business.title: AGIBOT Declares 2026 "Deployment Year One" at APC 2026, Accelerating the Era of Embodied AI Productivity
business.description: SHANGHAI, April 18, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- AGIBOT today outlined its long-term strategic vision for embodied intelligence at the AGIBOT Partner Conference (APC) 2026, declaring 2026 as the first year of large-scale commercial deployment of physical AI systems delivering measurable productivity gains.

business.title: Alberta commits to restore power line with Montana to capacity, potentially easing trade tension
business.description: Alberta has mandated a goal to restore the Montana-Alberta intertie to full capacity, whenever reasonable, potentially providing some relief to a trade irritant cited by U.S. President Donald Trump’s top trade representative last year.

business.title: Jet fuel supply warnings causing holidaymakers worry
business.description: Travel agents across Ireland are reporting an increase in the number of people contacting them with "worries about their holiday plans" amid warnings of a shortage of jet fuel.

business.title: Chinese carmakers rolling out cars with 'in-vehicle toilet.'
business.description: Seres, a Chinese automotive manufacturer, specializing in high-end, high-tech electric vehicles, has been granted a patent for in-vehicle toilets, which will allow travelers to use the restroom during road trips and camping trips.

science.title: Nature Might Pulse to a Universal Rhythm - 2 Beats Per Second
science.description: A surprising number of creatures across a wide swath of sizes, species, and communication methods all seem to time their signals to the same basic beat.

science.title: 'Tall waves moving in slow motion': Here's how oily oceans on Saturn's giant moon Titan may behave
science.description: The size of waves on alien worlds will depend as much on the characteristics of the liquid as well as the gravity.

science.title: Rare Comet Visible Over Connecticut This Month: When, Where To Look
science.description: Astronomers say Comet PanSTARRS R3 could be visible before sunrise, but timing and conditions will be key.

science.title: Volcanoes may have cooled Earth in a way scientists didn’t expect
science.description: Ash from ancient eruptions may have driven cooling on Earth by fertilizing oceans, boosting algal blooms, and pulling carbon from the air.

science.title: Scientists Discover a Hidden Giant Magma Reservoir Nestled Just 15 Kilometers Beneath Tuscany's Surface
science.description: A massive magma reservoir is secretly waiting beneath Italy’s peaceful landscape. With no eruptions in sight, scientists are scrambling to figure out why this enormous force remains dormant.

science.title: Life Beyond Biosignatures: A New Method In The Search For Life
science.description: Researchers from the Earth-Life Science Institute (ELSI) and National Institute for Basic Biology have developed a new method to detect extraterrestrial life without relying on traditional biosignatures. By modelling how life might spread between planets, they demonstrate that life could be detected through statistical patterns across planetary populations rather than on individual planets. This

ai.title: Cursor In Talks to Raise $2 Billion at Over $50 Billion Value
ai.description: Cursor, a leading artificial intelligence startup for coding, is in advanced talks with investors to raise about $2 billion in a funding round at a valuation of more than $50 billion, not including the investment, according to a person familiar with the matter.

ai.title: Palantir, Thales Among Companies Competing on FAA AI Tool
ai.description: The US Federal Aviation Administration has brought in Palantir Technologies Inc., Thales SA and Air Space Intelligence Inc. to compete on a new artificial intelligence tool for air traffic management, according to a person familiar with the matter.

ai.title: Authors are slamming Reese Witherspoon for telling followers to learn AI
ai.description: LOS ANGELES — Reese Witherspoon is hyping AI again, and American authors have a few thoughts. The Oscar-winning actor and producer, known for spotlighting women's voices through her famed book club, television and screen projects, may have been barking up the wrong tree when she told her social media followers that it was time to learn AI on Wednesday. "Well…I've decided it's TIME," she wrote ...

ai.title: White House meeting with Anthropic CEO over new AI model

ai.title: White House Chief of Staff to Meet With Anthropic CEO Over Its New AI Technology
ai.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — White House chief of staff Susie Wiles plans to sound out Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei about the artificial intelligence company's new Mythos model, which has attracted attention from the federal government for how it could transform national security and the economy.
